---
title: "Projects"
description: "Funded research projects at the Caravagna Lab"
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
---

<!-- Generated by scripts/build_projects_page.py. Edit content/projects/*.md. -->
::: {.projects-page}
::: {.projects-heading}
<p class="eyebrow">Research · Projects</p>

# Funded research

Our projects connect methodological development with biological questions, experimental design and clinical collaboration.
:::

<div class="projects-list">
<details class="project-card" open>
<summary>
<span class="project-index">01</span>
<span class="project-summary-main">
<span class="project-grant">AIRC BRIDGE · 2025–26</span>
<span class="project-title">AI for clonal evolution under therapy</span>
</span>
<span class="project-toggle" aria-hidden="true"></span>
</summary>
<div class="project-body">
<div class="project-visual"><img src="assets/project-airc-bridge.png" alt="Conceptual illustration of tumour clones evolving under treatment"></div>
<div class="project-copy">
<p class="project-lead">Characterising genotype and phenotype clonal evolution of response to therapy with Artificial Intelligence.</p>
<p>This project develops learning strategies to connect genomic alterations, cellular phenotypes and therapeutic response. The objective is to identify the evolutionary changes that allow resistant populations to emerge and persist.</p>
<dl class="project-facts"><div><dt>Funding</dt><dd>€100,000 · AIRC Foundation</dd></div><div><dt>Programme</dt><dd>BRIDGE Grant · Computational Biology</dd></div><div><dt>Leadership</dt><dd>Giulio Caravagna · Principal Investigator</dd></div><div><dt>Team</dt><dd>Cancer Data Science Laboratory</dd></div></dl>
<div class="project-outputs">
<p class="project-outputs-label">Example papers <span>changes on reload</span></p>
<div class="project-paper-list" data-project-papers="bridge"></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</details>

<details class="project-card" open>
<summary>
<span class="project-index">02</span>
<span class="project-summary-main">
<span class="project-grant">PRIN · 2023–25</span>
<span class="project-title">Machine learning for single-cell long-read sequencing</span>
</span>
<span class="project-toggle" aria-hidden="true"></span>
</summary>
<div class="project-body">
<div class="project-visual"><img src="assets/project-prin-long-read.png" alt="Conceptual illustration of single cells, long sequencing reads and machine learning"></div>
<div class="project-copy">
<p class="project-lead">Algorithms that learn tumour structure from long molecular measurements collected one cell at a time.</p>
<p>The project combines single-cell resolution with long-read sequencing to observe linked molecular events that shorter assays cannot resolve. We develop models for noisy, heterogeneous measurements and use them to reconstruct cellular populations and their evolutionary relationships.</p>
<dl class="project-facts"><div><dt>Funding</dt><dd>€250,000 total · approximately €140,000 to the lab</dd></div><div><dt>Programme</dt><dd>PRIN · Italian Ministry of University and Research · PE6</dd></div><div><dt>Leadership</dt><dd>Giulio Caravagna · Principal Investigator</dd></div><div><dt>Co-PI</dt><dd>Alberto Cazzaniga · Area Science Park</dd></div></dl>
<div class="project-outputs">
<p class="project-outputs-label">Example papers <span>changes on reload</span></p>
<div class="project-paper-list" data-project-papers="prin"></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</details>

<details class="project-card" open>
<summary>
<span class="project-index">03</span>
<span class="project-summary-main">
<span class="project-grant">AIRC MFAG · 2021–25</span>
<span class="project-title">Genotype, phenotype and therapeutic response</span>
</span>
<span class="project-toggle" aria-hidden="true"></span>
</summary>
<div class="project-body">
<div class="project-visual"><img src="assets/project-airc-mfag.png" alt="Conceptual illustration connecting tumour genotype, phenotype and treatment response"></div>
<div class="project-copy">
<p class="project-lead">Characterising genotype and phenotype clonal evolution of response to therapy with Artificial Intelligence.</p>
<p>This programme established our integrated approach to cancer evolution: combine sequencing, quantitative models and biological validation to understand how treatment reshapes heterogeneous tumours and selects resistant clones.</p>
<dl class="project-facts"><div><dt>Funding</dt><dd>€500,000 · AIRC Foundation</dd></div><div><dt>Programme</dt><dd>My First AIRC Grant · Computational Biology</dd></div><div><dt>Leadership</dt><dd>Giulio Caravagna · Principal Investigator</dd></div><div><dt>Team</dt><dd>Cancer Data Science Laboratory</dd></div></dl>
<div class="project-outputs">
<p class="project-outputs-label">Example papers <span>changes on reload</span></p>
<div class="project-paper-list" data-project-papers="mfag"></div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</details>
</div>
<script src="assets/projects-papers.js" defer></script>
:::
